sections.title: 1. N+1 Query Patterns
2. Unnecessary React Re-renders
3. Caching Opportunities
4. Memory Leaks
5. Redundant Computations
Priority Summary
sections.content:
| Issue | Impact | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| Sequential auth queries (`authRepository.ts`) | High (blocks every page load) | Low â€” `Promise.all` |
| O(nÂ·m) mapper filters (`dataRepository.ts`) | High as data grows | Low â€” pre-group with `Map` |
| Unmemoized `getVisible*` calls in `App.tsx` render | Medium â€” cascading re-renders | Low â€” wrap in `useMemo` |
| `buildStatements` recomputation per balance check | Medium | Medium â€” restructure batch calls |
| Un-debounced `localStorage` writes | Medium at scale | Low â€” debounce |
| `t` function identity in `useTranslation` | Low-medium | Low â€” `useCallback`/`useMemo` |

Since `App.tsx`, `dataRepository.ts`, and the view components are truncated in what I can see, I'd recommend a follow-up pass reading those files in full (especially the `useEffect` blocks in `App.tsx` and the rest of `dataRepository.ts`'s snapshot-loading logic) to confirm whether the `getVisible*` calls are actually unmemoized in JSX and whether the Supabase snapshot loader does its property/unit/issue joins in one batch or N+1-style per-property queries.

codeBlocks.code:
const t = useCallback((key: TranslationKey) => translations[key][language], [language]);
return useMemo(() => ({ language, setLanguage, t }), [language, t]);

// If called once per category in a loop, this redoes toCalculationUnits(units) every time
rules.forEach((rule) => calculateCategoryShare({ unit, category: rule.category, amount, units, rules }));
